category.name: zoo.window.recyclingMaterialInfo
category.item: Small box with a surprise
Go ahead and get a random selection of materials. This box will give you a small chance of getting rare materials.
Medium surprise box
Don't wait. This is your chance to get a random selection of materials. This box will give you a higher chance of getting rare materials.
Big box with a surprise
A box for professionals! Not only will you find a random selection of materials, but you will also have a very high chance of obtaining rare materials.
Wooden plank
This is not waste, but a valuable raw material: you can build various rooms with wooden planks.
Log
Sometimes even logs end up in the trash! Good wood is suitable for new projects.
Nail
Nails always come in handy. They hold everything together nicely.
Screw
A screw holds together what belongs together. All you need is a screwdriver!
Calculus
Even cavemen knew what could be done with stones. Sharpened stones are ideal for cutting.
Stone slab
It's incredible what zoo visitors throw away! A well-preserved stone slab will come in handy!
Sand
Sand is a valuable commodity. Even glass is made from sand. Did you know?
Dust sand
Fine, powdery sand is rare. Without the coarse grains, the sand can be as soft as cotton. It is also very important for your constructions.
A lump of earth
Good soil is full of nutrients and is a true natural product.
Topsoil
Fertile soil is enriched with organic matter, which provides nutrients for many purposes.
Seed
Plant seeds don't belong in the trash, but in the ground!
Seedling
This little seedling has roots. Pull it out of the trash and save its life!
Jacket
A raw material that can be easily melted. And hooray! Here you have a valuable raw material for many purposes.
Plastic
Molded plastic is the basis for many great products. Melt it, shape it, and reuse it!
Rope
Who dared to throw this away? The rope can be reused!
Rope
A useful piece of equipment for tying, wrapping or tightening.
Ore
Material enriched with metal and ore definitely does not belong in the trash.
Metal
Metal is a very valuable commodity that you should definitely hold on to.
Gum
Elastic, flexible and ideal for many creations.
Rubber
This original natural product is perfectly suited for further processing.
Gravel
These little stones are definitely not to be thrown away. You will need them for some construction projects.
Pebbles
These little pebbles have no value to many zoo visitors. But you can appreciate them!
Linen canvas
This material is very durable and woven from natural products.
Silk
So soft! A fine fabric obtained from the cocoon of the silkworm.
Water
Water is one of the basic materials of life. It occurs in different states: liquid, solid or vapor.
Drinking water
Drinking water is the most important commodity of all. Filtered water is often needed to produce the best products.
Clay
Clay is a mixture of sand and clay. It is one of the oldest building materials.
Brick
Many bricks are made from fired clay, while others are made from clay. They are great to work with and easy to store. How practical!
Glass
Previously, glass was made from sand. It was heated to a very high temperature, then blown and shaped.
Crystal
Lead oxide is added to the production of crystal. It is then polished to give the glass a subtle reflective effect.
Ornament
This gold ornament is ideal for decorating and embellishing many products.
Meadow flower
Delicate, fragrant meadow flowers are very pretty and useful for creating new decorations.
Waterlily
These unique, fragrant white flowers have a golden center. They are ideal for waterscapes.
Ice cube
Ice cubes are ideal building materials in cold regions, e.g. suitable for winter landscapes made of ice cubes!
Shaman mask
This ritual mask has religious origins. It is used to worship or ward off gods.
Desert cactus
Cacti adapt well to arid conditions and are excellent at retaining water.
Fossil
Fossils are petrified creatures whose bodies left an imprint in the sediment.
Indian cane
Bamboo cane is great as a decoration in Asian gardens.
Mud pit
A puddle! A hole full of mud - that's not at all unusual in a swamp. Marsh plants love wet environments!
Jungle stone
Jungle stone, wrapped in vine, is a strong building material.
Birch branch
Birch trees grow very quickly and are easily recognized by their easily peeling bark.
Snowman
Long carrot nose and black coal eyes: the snowman with a pot on his head looks very stylish indeed!
Heavenly pudding
Delicious chocolate topped with vanilla sauce and a cherry - a dessert that really tempts.
Tendril
Little tendrils sprout from the bare ground. How pretty!
Heat lamp
Keep your terrarium warm so that poikilotherm animals don't get cold.
Mountain
On the surface of the mountain range there are rocks and snow, and beneath the surface there are layers of soil.
Minerals
A bucket full of gems: something sparkles and shines in the rock.
Rotten branch
Rotten tree debris piles up on the forest floor and provides food for microorganisms such as fungi.
Scallop
A small treasure found on the beach and a special symbol for travelers.
Magic fly agaric
What secret does this magical glowing mushroom hide?
Buoy
These floating devices are essential to maintaining safety at sea.
Icicles
Ice is truly a "miracle" material. It is used to build houses, sculpt statues, or cool drinks. Be careful not to melt it.
Cannon-ball
Pirates love to shoot cannonballs at enemy ships, but most of them are at the bottom of the sea.
Coral
It looks like a plant, but it is an invertebrate. Its solid skeleton provides a home for many animals.
Coconut
The coconut is the most versatile fruit in the world. Its hard shell can be used for a variety of purposes. And it tastes good too!
